messages.content: please rephrase this paragraph 'As regard to serum levels of creatinine, the current experiment ACR-exposed animals recorded a significant increase indicating renal impairment and this in line with those of other studies (Karimani et al., 2019), (Shler et al., 2015) and (Pandey and Mishra (2004), who reported that creatinine is an excretion product of muscle activity, which circulates in blood. Its elimination is exclusively renal, so there is a direct correlation between creatinine levels and renal function. Most creatinine that is eliminated by the kidneys is freely filtered in renal glomeruli, and a small fraction is filtered by the tubular component, which is a good indicator of renal-glomerular function (Benziane et al., 2018). The increase of blood urea and serum creatinine is considered as a marker of kidney impairment according to (Karahan et al (2005). A study found that serum urea and creatinine levels were increased significantly in acrylamide-induced nephrotoxicity compared with controls (Ghorbel et al., 2016). ’

The serum levels of creatinine in the current experiment on ACR-exposed animals showed a significant increase, indicating renal impairment. This finding is consistent with previous studies conducted by Karimani et al. (2019), Shler et al. (2015), and Pandey and Mishra (2004), which also reported that creatinine is a byproduct of muscle activity that circulates in the blood and is eliminated solely by the kidneys. Therefore, there is a direct link between creatinine levels and renal function. Most of the creatinine eliminated by the kidneys is freely filtered in the renal glomeruli, with only a small fraction being filtered by the tubular component, making it a good indicator of renal-glomerular function, as stated by Benziane et al. (2018). Blood urea and serum creatinine levels in the blood are commonly used as markers for kidney impairment, according to Karahan et al. (2005). Ghorbel et al. (2016) conducted a study that found a significant increase in serum urea and creatinine levels in acrylamide-induced nephrotoxicity compared to the control group.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Oxidative stress is the main pathogenic mechanism through which ACR induces renal damage. Oxidative stress is a shift in the balance between oxidants and antioxidants in favour of oxidants Birben et al., (2012). Many researchers proved the oxidative stress role of ACR over the kidney Sengul et al., (2021). Acrylamide caused significant decreases in GSH and CAT activities, and significant increases in MDA concentration in kidney tissues. Our results were consistent with the findings of many other studies (Akbaribazm et al., (2021), Mohamed et al., (2020). ’

Renal damage induced by ACR primarily occurs through the main pathogenic mechanism of oxidative stress. Oxidative stress refers to an imbalance between oxidants and antioxidants, with an increase in oxidants, according to the research conducted by Birben et al. (2012). The role of oxidative stress in ACR-induced kidney damage has been supported by various researchers, including Sengul et al. (2021). Acrylamide has been found to cause significant decreases in the activities of GSH and CAT, while also leading to significant increases in MDA concentration in kidney tissues. Our findings align with those of numerous other studies, such as Akbaribazm et al. (2021) and Mohamed et al. (2020), which also observed similar alterations in oxidative stress markers in relation to acrylamide.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Lipid peroxidation increase is one of the basic mechanisms of acrylamide-induced oxidative kidney damage, and this damage is reduced by antioxidants, MDA is a toxic product of lipid peroxidation Elhelaly et al., (2019), Erdemli et al., (2019). As can be seen from our experimental results, the amount of GSH was found to be low in the acrylamide group, where the amount of MDA was found high. Supporting this finding, in the study by Alturfan et al., (2012). ’

An elevation in lipid peroxidation is a key process underlying the oxidative damage to the kidneys caused by acrylamide, and this damage can be mitigated by antioxidants. MDA, a harmful byproduct of lipid peroxidation, has been demonstrated to be involved in this process, as noted by Elhelaly et al. (2019) and Erdemli et al. (2019). Our experimental results reflect a reduced level of GSH in the acrylamide group, accompanied by an increased level of MDA. This finding is supported by the research conducted by Alturfan et al. (2012).

please rephrase this paragraph 'Concerning the histopathological alterations, the present work revealed that ACR-exposed animals showed congestion of the glomerular capillary tuft, lost their brush borders, infiltration of inflammatory cells, vacuolar degenerative, focal hemorrhages, necrosis of tubular in renal parenchyma. These observations are consistent with those. (Karimani et al., (2019), Sengul et al., (2021), Susan et al., (2003), Nasralla et al., (2021). ’

The histopathological changes observed in the present study indicate that animals exposed to ACR exhibited various alterations in the renal tissue. These alterations included congestion of the glomerular capillary tuft, loss of brush borders, presence of infiltrating inflammatory cells, vacuolar degeneration, focal hemorrhages, and tubular necrosis within the renal parenchyma. These findings align with previous studies conducted by Karimani et al. (2019), Sengul et al. (2021), Susan et al. (2003), and Nasralla et al. (2021).

please rephrase this paragraph 'Antioxidants have a role in nephroprotection as they cause apoptosis of myofibroblasts and regenerate epithelial cells (Wojcikowski et al 2009). Cinnamon is commonly used as an herbal medication. This plant usually grows in Asia and Australia.  The medical benefits of this herb are linked to its active components. It is found from the analyses of cinnamon that it contains different materials like cumarin, cinamyldehyde and cinnamic acid (Lim and Ko, 2022).  The plant has antiallergic, antiviral, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as an effect on heart disease treatments and diabetes (Shen et al., 2012).  Many recent studies have proved cinnamon’s therapeutic and preventive potential against a range of infections linked to oxidative stress (Li et al., 2019., Salman et al., 2021, Alshahrani et al., 2021). ’

Antioxidants play a role in protecting the kidneys by inducing apoptosis of myofibroblasts and promoting the regeneration of epithelial cells, as demonstrated by Wojcikowski et al. (2009). Cinnamon, a widely used herbal medication, is commonly found in Asia and Australia. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its active components, including cumarin, cinnamaldehyde, and cinnamic acid, as identified through cinnamon analyses conducted by Lim and Ko (2022). Cinnamon possesses several beneficial effects, such as being antiallergic, antiviral, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. It has also been associated with potential benefits in treatments for heart disease and diabetes, as noted by Shen et al. (2012). Recent studies have provided evidence of cinnamon's therapeutic and preventive potential against various infections associated with oxidative stress, as documented by Li et al. (2019), Salman et al. (2021), and Alshahrani et al. (2021).

please rephrase this paragraph 'Cinnamon, a commonly used herb, has antioxidative properties which guard human kidneys from injuries caused by diabetes and various toxins (Mhammad et al 2015). 
(Gunawardena et al., 2015), (Barboza et al., 2018) and (sparsh et al., 2019), reported that nephroprotective effect of cinnamon might be due to the presence of cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.
Cinnamon was reported to be agood antioxidant with successful nephroprotrctive action in paracetamol-induced nephrotoxicity in rats Quamuddin et al., (2021), Hussain et al., (2019), in gentamycin induced nephrotoxicity Tanomand and Najafian, (2013), Ullah et al., (2013) and Elkomy et al., (2020), in cypermethrin induced nephrotic disorders Sakr and Albarakai (2014), negative effect of fluoxetine drug on renal tissue Hashem et al., (2022). Significant decreases in levels of creatinine and urea and an improvement in the histological structure of the kidney were reported by those researchers. ’

Cinnamon, an herb commonly used in various applications, possesses antioxidative properties that can protect the kidneys from damage induced by diabetes and various toxins, according to Mhammad et al. (2015). The nephroprotective effects of cinnamon have been attributed to the presence of cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, as highlighted by Gunawardena et al. (2015), Barboza et al. (2018), and Sparsh et al. (2019). Several studies have reported the beneficial nephroprotective action of cinnamon as an antioxidant in different models, such as paracetamol-induced nephrotoxicity in rats (Quamuddin et al., 2021; Hussain et al., 2019), gentamicin-induced nephrotoxicity (Tanomand and Najafian, 2013; Ullah et al., 2013; Elkomy et al., 2020), cypermethrin-induced nephrotic disorders (Sakr and Albarakai, 2014), and the negative impact of fluoxetine drug on renal tissue (Hashem et al., 2022). These researchers reported significant reductions in creatinine and urea levels, along with an improvement in the histological structure of the kidney.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Morgan et al., 2014 announced that the protective action of Cinnamon extract against renal oxidative injury is due to its positive role on the antioxidant enzymes and inhibitory action on ROS synthesis and revealed that the great content of polyphenolic substances in Cinnamon extract qualified it to serve as a nutritional supplement of natural antioxidants. Similarly, previous researchers have recorded the antioxidant action of Cinnamon in vitro and in vivo (Shobana and Naidu 2000), (Eidi et al., 2012).’

According to Morgan et al. (2014), Cinnamon extract has a protective effect against renal oxidative injury, which can be attributed to its ability to enhance antioxidant enzymes and inhibit the synthesis of reactive oxygen species (ROS). The study also indicated that Cinnamon extract contains a high concentration of polyphenolic compounds, making it suitable as a natural nutritional supplement with antioxidant properties. Similar findings have been reported by previous researchers, both in vitro and in vivo, such as Shobana and Naidu (2000) and Eidi et al. (2012), who also observed the antioxidant action of Cinnamon.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Roussel et al., 2009 demonstrated that Cinnamon had antioxidant activity by removal of free radicals and inhibition of 5-lipoxygenase enzyme, in addition to decrease lipid peroxidation and increase total antioxidant power as mentioned by (RanJbar et al., 2006).
Mishra et al. (2010) reported that cinnamon protects the kidney by reducing glomerular expansion and decreasing the tubular dilations.
Rosemary (Rosmarinus officinalis L., Lamiaceae) is a woody perennial herb, native to the Mediterranean region.’

Cinnamon has been shown to possess antioxidant activity through the neutralization of free radicals and inhibition of the 5-lipoxygenase enzyme. Additionally, it has been found to decrease lipid peroxidation and increase the total antioxidant power, as demonstrated by Roussel et al. (2009) and mentioned by RanJbar et al. (2006). Mishra et al. (2010) reported that cinnamon provides kidney protection by reducing glomerular expansion and decreasing tubular dilations. Rosemary (Rosmarinus officinalis L., Lamiaceae) is a perennial herb with a woody structure and is originally from the Mediterranean region.

please rephrase this paragraph 'The leaves of rosemary are commonly used for flavoring foods as a condiment, but this plant has also been widely used for different medicinal purposes. In traditional medicine, rosemary has been used as a stimulant and mild analgesic, and it has been considered as one of the most effective herbs for treating headaches, poor circulation, inflammatory diseases, and physical and mental fatigue. Rosemary has also been used empirically as a choleretic and hepatoprotective agent in folk medicine (Yu et al., 2013).
Most pharmacological effects of rosemary are the consequence of high antioxidant activity of its main chemical constituents, the potent antioxidant properties of rosemary have been mainly attributed to its major diterpenes, carnosol and carnosic acid, as well as to the essential oil components (Ngo et al., 2011).
’

Rosemary leaves are widely recognized for their common use as a food condiment to enhance flavors. However, this plant has also gained significant attention for its diverse medicinal purposes. In traditional medicine, rosemary has been valued as a stimulant and mild analgesic, often regarded as an effective remedy for ailments such as headaches, poor circulation, inflammatory disorders, and both physical and mental fatigue. Additionally, rosemary has been empirically utilized in folk medicine as a choleretic and hepatoprotective agent (Yu et al., 2013). The pharmacological effects of rosemary can largely be attributed to the remarkable antioxidant activity of its primary chemical constituents. The potent antioxidant properties of rosemary are primarily associated with its major diterpenes, carnosol and carnosic acid, as well as the components found in its essential oil (Ngo et al., 2011).

please rephrase this paragraph 'Due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial activity (Bozin et al., 2007), rosemary essential oil is capable to extend the shelf-life of food products and maintain their quality during storage. Therefore, it has already being used as a biopreservative in food industry (Ojeda et al., 2013) and (Kadri et al., 2011).
Virk et al., (2013) showed that co-treatment with rosemary leaf extract as an antioxidant alleviated the malondialdehyde (MDA) levels, and increased superoxide dismutase (SOD) and catalase (CAT) activities and also restored cadmium-induced cellular disorganization in the liver and kidney.
The supportive role of rosemary extract treatment in lead-induced hepatotoxicity and nephrotoxicity in rabbits was investigated by Mohamed et al., (2016) and they assured that rosemary pretreatment could protect the liver and kidney from lead injury by radical-scavenging and antioxidant activities.'

Rosemary essential oil possesses antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, as demonstrated by Bozin et al. (2007). These qualities enable it to prolong the shelf life of food products and maintain their overall quality during storage. Consequently, rosemary essential oil has been utilized as a biopreservative in the food industry, as noted by Ojeda et al. (2013) and Kadri et al. (2011).

Virk et al. (2013) found that co-treatment with rosemary leaf extract as an antioxidant effectively reduced malondialdehyde (MDA) levels, increased the activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD) and catalase (CAT), and restored cellular disorganization induced by cadmium in the liver and kidney.

The potential of rosemary extract treatment as a supportive therapy for lead-induced hepatotoxicity and nephrotoxicity in rabbits was investigated by Mohamed et al. (2016). Their study revealed that pretreatment with rosemary extract had the ability to safeguard the liver and kidney from lead-related damage through its radical-scavenging and antioxidant activities.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Azab et al., (2014) noticed that rosemary ameliorated the nephrotoxicity of gentamycin in guinea pigs and improved the structural changes in the kidney and declined the blood urea, creatinine and uric acid.
Carnosic acid enhanced the GSH level and the antioxidant enzyme activities including SOD, CAT and GSH-Px. These effects were attributed to its potent antioxidant and anti-apoptotic properties (Sahu et al., 2011).
Rosemary could suppress nephrotoxic effects of CCl4 in mice through the prevention of lipid peroxidation Hamed et al., (2020).
Tavafi et al., (2011) concluded significant decrease in glomerular hypertrophy, glomerulosclerosis and attenuated urea and creatinine.
Ahmed and Abdella (2010) reported that rosemary prevented histopathological lesions and oxidative stress induced by doxorubicin in liver, kidney and heart of mice. 
Rosmarinus officinalis prevented histopathological lesions and oxidative stress induced by (Li2CO3) in kidneys and testis tissues (Mwaheb et al., 2016).'

Rosemary was found to improve the nephrotoxicity of gentamicin in guinea pigs and alleviate the structural changes observed in the kidney, resulting in decreased levels of blood urea, creatinine, and uric acid, as observed by Azab et al. (2014).

The potent antioxidant and anti-apoptotic properties of carnosic acid were associated with enhancements in glutathione (GSH) levels and the activities of antioxidant enzymes, including superoxide dismutase (SOD), catalase (CAT), and glutathione peroxidase (GSH-Px), as highlighted by Sahu et al. (2011).

The nephrotoxic effects of CCl4 in mice were suppressed by rosemary through the prevention of lipid peroxidation, as demonstrated by Hamed et al. (2020).

Tavafi et al. (2011) observed a significant reduction in glomerular hypertrophy and glomerulosclerosis, accompanied by attenuated levels of urea and creatinine.

Rosemary was also found to protect against histopathological lesions and oxidative stress induced by doxorubicin in the liver, kidney, and heart of mice, according to Ahmed and Abdella (2010).

The preventive effects of Rosmarinus officinalis on histopathological lesions and oxidative stress
